code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Complete failure to perform core function despite proper use
    - Multi-purchase defects with safety hazards
    - Unusable design requiring fundamental workarounds
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Requires constant adjustments for basic functionality
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - Partial functionality with recurring frustration
    - Regret statements with cost-benefit justification

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual compromises
    - "Works if..." conditional recommendations
    - Adequate for limited use cases without endorsement
    - Technical competence with notable tradeoffs

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Explicit star deduction ("only 4 because...")
    - Usage limitations affecting functionality (e.g. capacity)
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Requires break-in period for optimal performance

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Concise praise without negatives ("Works perfectly")
    - 3+ complementary strengths (function/design/durability)
    - Close comparisons to top brands with satisfaction
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Long-term validation with sustained performance
    - Core excellence outweighing setup/learning curve

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Nearly matches X" comparisons →5 if user recommends
    - Material maintenance needs →5 if non-functional
    - Praise + functional flaw →4 / + non-functional →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial success
    - Brief effectiveness praise →5 even if concise
    - Core function achieved post-setup →5 if flawless
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override positives →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed - deep pockets fit any mattress"
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise without negatives →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Stasher bag perfect for snacks. Don't overstuff - affects seal"
<Reasoning>Functional limitation despite praise →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Vibrant colors need ironing but worth it for durability"
<Reasoning>Non-functional criticism with core praise →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Constantly retighten screws - not worth the effort"
<Reasoning>Excessive maintenance →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Good for light use. Thin material concerns for heavy loads"
<Reasoning>Adequate with notable compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Assembly took muscle but now handles bumps perfectly"
<Reasoning>Setup difficulty outweighed by core excellence →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "These work after washing but edges stay stiff"
<Reasoning>Lingering functional drawback →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Innovative concept fails - parts detach during use"
<Reasoning>Core usability failure →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (28 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 15
  gold 5 -> 4: 4
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 5 -> 3: 1
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I keep saying I won't get anything else from DII because the quality has been so lacking in the past but these Hanukkah themed kitchen towels were just the right blend of decorative and functional. The size is nice as is the decorative element; these..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I got this low profile box spring for my office to use along with a Sealy Cocoon mattress, which is soft. After putting this one together, I will need to add a piece of wood to support the mattress so that it doesn't dip between the slats. The slats ..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "This is an ok bin. It holds its shape fairly well and is nice and sturdy. There is no side support, so you can't stack something on the top. Color is a nice light pink-nothing too flashy."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 28 errors, 19 borderline out of 80 examples."}
